meta.title: Public-Readiness Audit (read-only)

git.note: Branch reported 3 commits behind origin/main at scan time; audit based on local tree.

scan_methods: git status; git branch --show-current; git ls-files (748 paths)
ripgrep-style searches: token/secret patterns, PII path hints, internal URLs, binary extensions
no gitleaks/trufflehog in PATH; no third-party secret scanner executed
meta.verdict_rationale: P1-PII (F002) mitigated. Root LICENSE is proprietary (All rights reserved); repo may be public for transparency — not Open Source. Before flipping GitHub visibility, run a dependency license compliance review (copyleft / distribution obligations).
open_prerequisites_before_public_visibil: Dependency and transitive license audit (ensure no obligation conflicts with proprietary distribution model).

remediation.policy: Public visibility allowed; not OSS; third-party components remain under their own licenses; do not modify third-party license terms in-repo.

findings.summary: No evidence of committed live API keys, GitHub PATs, PEM/private keys, or gcp/aws-style long tokens in tracked files
findings.evidence: Searches for ghp_, github_pat_, AIza..., BEGIN PRIVATE KEY, SK-..., AKIA... returned no matches; .env and google-services files not in tree
findings.risk: If such material appeared in history, it would be out of scope of this file-only scan
findings.notes: Ongoing: run dedicated secret scan on full git history before first public push if not already done

findings.summary: Echte E-Mail-Adresse und Windows-Benutzerpfad in mehreren Markdown-Dateien
findings.evidence: Former occurrence of a real e-mail inside local path strings and multiple C:\Users\... absolute paths; redacted to <LOCAL_REPO_PATH> (EMM-223).
findings.risk: Was: Personenbezug und Rechnerstruktur; now mitigated in listed files.

findings.summary: Root-LICENSE und README: proprietary / All rights reserved (nicht Open Source)
findings.risk: Vor oeffentlicher Sichtbarkeit: Abhaengigkeitslizenzen pruefen (Copyleft, Weitergabe, Offenlegung).
findings.notes: Project code: All rights reserved. Third-party deps: unchanged; subject to their licenses. SPDX in pubspec optional follow-up.

findings.risk: Fehlende Erwartungshaltung an Meldung von Sicherheitsproblemen und Beitraege; untypisch fuer oeffentliche GitHub-Projekte
findings.recommended_action: KEEP (add templates in follow-up) / NEEDS_LEGAL_REVIEW for SECURITY contact wording

findings.summary: Verknuepfung operativer Infrastruktur: GitHub-Org/Repo-Handle und viele linear.app-Links in Doku/Workflows
findings.risk: Enthuellt Organisations-Workspace, Issue-Nummern und Projektnamen; meist kein Geheimnis, aber strategische Transparenz
findings.recommended_action: KEEP oder REDACT je nach Kommunikationsziel; nicht security-kritisch per se

findings.summary: GitHub Actions nutzen repository secrets (LLM, Linear); CI erzwingt Linear-Ticket-Referenz in PRs
findings.risk: Geheimnisse in Fork-PR-Workflows sind standardmaessig nicht an Forks gepaart; PR-Governance kann externe Contributor ohne Linear-Zugang ausschliessen oder frustrieren
findings.recommended_action: KEEP (workflows) / dokumentieren in CONTRIBUTING (Folge-PR) welche Workflows von Fork-PRs erwarten, zu skippen sind, oder was Linear-Pflicht bedeutet

findings.risk: Hinweis, dass self-hosted Runner genutzt werden; bei public wird das sichtbar; niedrig solange nur manuell dispatch
findings.recommended_action: KEEP oder MOVE_PRIVATE if repo wird rein community-gehostet ohne private Runner

findings.summary: Beispiel-/Demo-E-Mails in Code (z. B. demo@emma.de) und oeffentlich erwartbare Platzhalter
Binaerdateien: App-Icons, favicon, gradle-wrapper.jar; keine Zip/Dumps/SQLite im Sample
findings.risk: Normal fuer Flutter-Projekte; Wrapper-JAR ist ueblich
